column1: How does CodeDeploy know a deployment is in progress?
column2: The CodeDeploy agent on instances sends progress reports to the CodeDeploy service (service doesn't query instances directly)

column1: When does the ApplicationStop lifecycle event run in CodeDeploy?
column2: Before new deployment files download, so it will NOT run the first time a deployment occurs on an instance

column1: What happens when multiple sources are configured in a CodePipeline?
column2: The pipeline is triggered when ANY source is updated

column1: What is the most cost-effective way to manage code and large binary objects in a deployment?
column2: Use CodeCommit repository for code and S3 archive for binary objects (Git storage expensive for large binaries)

column1: What deployment strategy ensures no downtime and automated switching between groups?
column2: Blue/green deployment - spins up new compute, confirms health, then switches load balancer to new deployment

column1: What role downloads the deployment bundle from S3 in CodeDeploy?
column2: The CodeDeploy agent on each instance (not the CodePipeline service role)

column1: How do you specify output artifacts in CodeBuild?
column2: Use the files directive in the buildspec.yml specification to indicate files from build environment

column1: What is the most secure way to store secrets for CodeBuild?
column2: Store encrypted secrets in AWS Secrets Manager (not in custom environment, S3, or source control)

column1: What is the only action type for Lambda functions in CodePipeline?
column2: Invoke action type (Lambda can only execute as part of pipeline with Invoke)

column1: Can pipeline stages support multiple actions?
column2: Yes, pipeline actions can occur both in series and in parallel within the same stage

column1: Can appspec.yml files directly control infrastructure?
column2: No, appspec.yml cannot directly control infrastructure (it controls deployment lifecycle)

column1: What authentication methods does CodeCommit support?
column2: IAM credentials for both SSH and HTTPS (fully compatible with existing Git tools)

column1: Can SNS notifications be configured when a commit is made to CodeCommit?
column2: No, SNS notifications cannot be configured for commits to a repository

column1: What action types support CodeBuild projects in CodePipeline?
column2: Build and Test action types

column1: What is the optimal way to automate build and deploy with AWS services?
column2: Link distinct CodeBuild and CodeDeploy projects into a continuous delivery pipeline via CodePipeline

column1: What type of deployments do on-premises instances support in CodeDeploy?
column2: Only in-place deployments (AWS cannot create/destroy infrastructure in customer data centers)

column1: Does CodeDeploy remove files it doesn't manage by default?
column2: No, CodeDeploy will not remove files that it does not manage (maintains list of managed files on instance)

column1: Can Lambda function versions be modified after publishing?
column2: No, function versions cannot be modified after publishing (use aliases to point to different versions)

column1: What is the minimum number of stages required in a CodePipeline?
column2: 2 stages - must contain a source stage and at least one build or deploy stage

column1: Why might a CodeDeploy deployment fail when accessing artifacts?
column2: Instances in the deployment group need access to the pipeline's S3 bucket where artifacts are stored

column1: Does the CodePipeline service role download S3 bundles?
column2: No, the CodeDeploy agent on instances downloads bundles, not the CodePipeline service

column1: Are FIFO/LIFO valid pipeline action configurations?
column2: No, FIFO/LIFO are not valid pipeline action configurations

column1: Can Lambda deployment aliases be overwritten?
column2: No, aliases are pointers to function versions and cannot be overwritten

column1: Does CodeDeploy support in-place deployments for Lambda?
column2: No, Lambda does not support in-place deployments because published versions cannot be updated

column1: What happens during a blue/green deployment with Auto Scaling?
column2: CodeDeploy spins up new instances in alternate target group, ensures health, then switches load balancer
